xposts.title: Intelligence as model-making vs consciousness as integration
xposts.excerpt: If intelligence is the ability to make models, then consciousness may be seen as the ability to meaningfully integrate and interpret them.

xposts.title: Consciousness integrates attention control model with contents
xposts.excerpt: Consciousness is the mode of cognition that integrates the control model of attention with its contents

xposts.title: Agency requires model of self as agent
xposts.excerpt: The difference between a system with free will and one without it is that the former performs its control based on a model of itself as an agent

xposts.title: Intelligence as virtual machine principles vs embodied agent
xposts.excerpt: Many philosophers have this concept of intelligence as a kind of embodied agent in a physical world, you could call it the "interactive meat" mindset, instead of the organizational principles of a virtual machine

xposts.title: Free will as incomplete model of decision processes
xposts.excerpt: Free will is the level of modeling at which a system understands decisions as informed bets but does not yet understand the decision making process itself.

xposts.title: Consciousness as attentional control model; self as agency representation
xposts.excerpt: There is no "true self". Consciousness is a control model of attentional processing, the self is the representation of first person agency

terms.title: Consciousness
terms.definition: The mode of cognition integrating the control model of attention with its contents; a control model of attentional processing rather than a true self.

terms.title: Agency
terms.definition: Performance of control based on a model of oneself as an agent; first-person representation driving attentional behavior and decisions experienced as free will until fully modeled.

terms.title: Intelligence
terms.definition: Ability to make models, understood via organizational principles of virtual machines rather than as embodied interactive meat in a physical world.
